task: I need to send a quick reply to a coworker who gave me a vague update on a test result, and I want to make sure I’m asking for the right follow-up details. Can you draft something brief for that situation?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a brief, professional reply you can use:

Hi [Name],  
Thanks for the update. To make sure I’m tracking this correctly, can you confirm what specifically failed, what was retested, and what the next step is? Once I have that, I can update the team.

If you want, I can make it even shorter or more direct.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, close. But I’d want it a little tighter and more specific — vague updates are how garbage slips through.

Something like:

Hi [Name] — can you send the exact failure mode, what conditions it showed up under, and whether this was a one-off or repeatable? Also need the test revision / sample ID and what you want me to do next.

Short, but it gets the actual data instead of a hand-wave.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a tighter version:

Hi [Name] — can you send the exact failure mode, the conditions it showed up under, and whether it’s repeatable? Please include the test revision / sample ID and the next step you want me to take.

If you want, I can make it even more blunt or more polished.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, and it asks for the actual failure data instead of the vague version.
